Member-only story

6 min read
Just now

--

Claude Design is a very useful tool for quick prototyping. It offers a lot of great features, such as the ability to use your design system when crafting design prototypes or relying on the full power of the latest Claude Opus model when building a new design.
Recently, Claude Design got a major update. With this upgrade, Anthropic solved its perhaps the most painful problem: token usage. Now, Claude Design uses the same token quota as regular Claude.
When reviewing Claude Design, the design community typically focuses on its ability to create web and mobile prototypes. But Claude Design has a very interesting and super useful feature: Slides.
And in this article, I want to create a new slide deck using Slides, showing why and how you can use.
Why generate Slides in Claude Design?
Because we already have many tools for creating slides available on the market (including Figma Slides), it can be unclear why anyone should use Slides in Claude Design.
